mission: Reduce the remaining Paper 35 framework debts to exact externally-theoremic target objects

theorem_1_mean_baryon_mass_target.status: derived / exact target from the closed late-time eta theorem
theorem_1_mean_baryon_mass_target.statement: Any future internal color/confinement theorem that claims to close the baryon mass debt must reproduce this target mass on the active branch.

theorem_2_b_l_precursor_target.status: derived / exact target using the external Standard-Model sphaleron theorem
theorem_2_b_l_precursor_target.statement: Any source-era mechanism that closes the observed baryon asymmetry through Standard-Model sphalerons must produce this B-L precursor while sphalerons remain active.

theorem_3_freezeout_target.status: derived / exact target using the external Poplawski Hehl-Datta forward map
theorem_3_freezeout_target.statement: Any source-era torsion-baryogenesis mechanism using Poplawski's forward asymmetry law must select this freeze-out scale to reproduce the closed late-time eta.

reduction_theorem.statement: Under the current framework closure for late-time eta and the external Standard-Model/QCD/EC theorems cited below, the remaining Paper 35 debts reduce exactly to three target objects: (i) the mean baryon mass target m_bar,target, (ii) the precursor asymmetry target Y_(B-L), and (iii) the freeze-out target T_f. No further ambiguity remains at the level of targets; only the internal sector that could generate them remains open.
still_missing_internal_sectors: an SU(3)_c / confinement / dimensional-transmutation theorem sector
a baryon-separation or baryon-number-violating source operator
